steps.explanation: Cosecant is the reciprocal of sine, so we evaluate it by first finding the relevant sine value.
Substituting $\theta=30^\circ$ reduces the task to finding one over $\sin 30^\circ$.

steps.description: Recall the exact value of sin 30
steps.explanation: From the half-equilateral triangle, the side opposite $30^\circ$ is half the hypotenuse, giving $\sin 30^\circ=\tfrac12$.
Replacing $\sin 30^\circ$ by $\tfrac12$ leaves one divided by a half.

steps.description: Simplify the reciprocal of a fraction
steps.explanation: Dividing by $\tfrac12$ is the same as multiplying by $2$, so the reciprocal of a half is two.
A sine never exceeds $1$ in size, so its reciprocal is at least $1$ in size. The answer $2$ fits this comfortably.
This exact value is what the question requires, and notice it equals $\sec 60^\circ$ because $\sin 30^\circ=\cos 60^\circ$.
